title: Medical Care Expense Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Medical Care Expense Assistance 26 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter AIDS/HIV Breast Cancer Cancer Cervical Cancer Stillbirth Women 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management to individuals living with HIV and AIDS. This program provides expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care appointments, transitional housing and rent payment assistance, disease management education, referrals to resources for basic needs, medical and dental services, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 342-5088 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CAREASSIST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides assistance paying for insurance premiums, prescriptions, and medical service co-pays. Assists with 100% payments for a limited number of medical services and medications necessary for HIV treatment for uninsured persons. 800 NE Oregon Street #1105 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: HIV positive Oregonians with income up to 550% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm by appointment only Email (971) 673-0144 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DESCHUTES COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV/AIDS CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management to individuals living with HIV and AIDS. This program provides expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing and rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 2577 NE Courtney Drive Bend, OR 97701 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8:00am-5pm Email (541) 322-7155 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HAYDEN'S HELPING HANDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance to families who deliver a stillbirth. Financial assistance is for the delivery bills only; does not offer financial assistance for any other medical bills. PO Box 2041 Clackamas, OR 97015 Eligibility: Families who deliver a stillbirth, at least 20 weeks gestation Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 504-0512 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOOD RIVER COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT HIV/AIDS CASE MANAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management to individuals living with HIV and AIDS. This program provides HIV testing, expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing and rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 1109 June Street Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 386-1115 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INFORMATIONAL RECORD CHARITY CARE HOSPITAL EXPENSES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Federal and state laws require hospitals to provide certain types of care for free or at a lower cost if the patient cannot afford medical treatment. More Details KAISER PERMANENTE HEALTH CARE KAISER PERMANENTE NORTHEAST REGION MEDICAL GROUP MEDICAL FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers temporary financial assistance and charity care to low-income, uninsured, and under-served patients of Kaiser Permanente providers. Also provides financial counseling for outstanding Kaiser Permanente medical and pharmacy bills if patient is unemployed or without other significant income. 500 NE Multnomah Boulevard Suite 100 Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Patients of Kaiser Permanente Medical Group who have an outstanding balance with Kaiser Permanente and an annual gross household income at or below 400% of the Federal Poverty Level. Must have exhausted all other financial assistance and insurance options. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (503) 813-2000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH ALBANY HIV/AIDS RAPID TESTING AND PREVENTION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential rapid testing for HIV/AIDS, syphilis, and hepatitis C. Offers prevention education and treatment support. Assists with access to HIV medications, transportation to medical appointments, transitional housing, disease management education, and referrals for basic needs, employment, and substance use treatment. 1050 SW 7th Avenue Albany, OR 97321 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV/AIDS Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-noon and 1pm-5pm Email (541) 967-3888 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MESOTHELIOMA PROGNOSIS NETWORK Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers specialized information and referral services, resource navigation assistance, and online support groups. Provides limited financial assistance to people with mesothelioma. Also assists veterans get VA Disability Compensation for asbestos-related illnesses. 200 Continental Drive Suite 401 #504 Newark, DE 19713 Eligibility: People impacted by mesothelioma and asbestos exposure Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (800) 615-2270 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST FAMILY SERVICES WOMEN'S HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Educates, identifies, and assists women in accessing mammogram vouchers. Provides care coordination and navigation of ScreenWise services. 6200 SE King Road Portland, OR 97222 Eligibility: Women age 40 and older who have not had a mammogram in the past year or at all ScreenWise services: Women age 50 and older and needing breast or cervical cancer screening or diagnostic services. Must have a household income at or below 250% of Federal Poverty Level, and must either have no health insurance or have health insurance, but not enough to cover their needs. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4:30pm Email (503) 546-6377 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPERATION HOMEFRONT CRITICAL FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides critical financial assistance for documented needs. Types of assistance include: auto payment and repair, child and dependent care, critical baby items, dental, essential home items, food assistance, home repairs, medical, moving and relocation, rent and mortgage, travel and transportation, utilities, and vision care. Home repairs can be considered for all post-9/11 wounded service members. Repairs versus replacements can be considered for deployed eligible service members. Number of times a person can receive assistance: -Active duty member with a rank of E1-E6 may receive 3 total lifetime assists -Currently deployed on Title 10 orders and/are receiving hazardous duty pay may receive 5 total lifetime assists -Documented injury, illness, or wound during post 9/11 service may receive 5 total lifetime assists The number of months of assistance that can be received varies on the type of assistance being requested 17319 San Pedro Avenue Suite 505 San Antonio, TX 78232 Eligibility: Must fit one of three criteria: 1) Wounded, Ill, or Injured Criteria Actively served in the United States military post 9/11 AND suffered a post 9/11 wound, illness, or injury in the line of duty. If discharged, must have Honorable or General (Under Honorable Conditions) discharge within 10 years of discharge. 2) Deployed Criteria A service member of rank E1-E6 with DEERS (Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System) eligible/legal dependents AND one of the following criteria's met: -Currently deployed to an overseas location and receiving Hostile Fire Pay -Assigned to a ship that has been at sea for 30+ days and is en route to theater, forward deployed, or otherwise on a mission status, not a training status -On unaccompanied, overseas PCS orders and receiving Hostile Fire Pay -A National Guard member or Reservist on Title 10 orders (not Title 32) who either: Will deploy to an overseas Hostile Fire Pay theater in the next 90 days or has returned from an overseas Hostile Fire Pay theater within the past 180 days 3) General Criteria -An active duty service member of rank E1-E6 with DEERS eligible/legal dependents Hours: Helpline: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week, leave a message and a coordinator will call back Intake Coordinators: Monday-Friday 7am-3pm Email (210) 659-7756 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Operates a call-in center for family members of children and youth with special needs and chronic health conditions (developmental and emotional disabilities, behavioral problems, and chronic physical illnesses) and professionals who serve them. The center specializes in providing information and resources related to health information, health care access, and special needs systems and supports. All calls are confidential. Families may also request to be placed on an email list to receive monthly correspondence about issues of importance to children and youth with special needs in Oregon. 707 SW Gaines Street Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Family members of individuals age 26 and younger with special physical, emotional, behavioral, or mental health needs, including those who are at risk or suspected of having a special need. The child/youth does not have to have a diagnosed condition. Does not require documentation to prove citizenship to receive services. Hours: Message line available 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (833) 990-9930 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION BREAST AND CERVICAL CANCER TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Medical benefit program through OHP for individuals needing treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer who do not have health insurance coverage. Individuals may ask their provider to refer them to the program. Applicant 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: People under age 65 who live in Oregon and: -Need treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer -Do not have minimum essential coverage for treatment -Do not qualify for any other Medicaid programs -Household income at or below 250% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) Hours: Vary by site Email (800) 699-9075 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON LIONS SIGHT AND HEARING FOUNDATION PATIENT CARE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance for eye and ear surgeries as follows: -Vision: cataract surgery, corneal transplant, enucleation, retinal injection, autologous serum eye drops, dry eye treatment, medical contact lenses, corneal crosslinking, insurance co-pays and deductibles, prosthetic eye, pterygium surgery, and more. -Hearing: adenoidectomy, tympanoplasty, mastoidectomy, myringotomy, expenses related to implantable hearing devices, and more. 1010 NW 22nd Avenue Suite 144 Portland, OR 97210 Eligibility: Inviduals who have a gross household income at or below 200% of the FPL and have exhausted all other resources to pay for treatment Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm. Closed holidays. Email (503) 413-7399 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE COMMUNITY HEALTH ASHLAND HEALTH CENTER BCCTP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Medical benefit program through OHP for individuals needing treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer who do not have health insurance coverage. Individuals may ask their provider to refer them to the program. 99 Central Avenue Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: People under age 65 who live in Oregon and: -Need treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer -Do not have minimum essential coverage for treatment -Do not qualify for any other Medicaid programs -Household income at or below 250% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) -Anyone with a diagnosis of breast or cervical cancer to apply. The program does not look at gender identity or expression. Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 773-3863 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE COMMUNITY HEALTH BUTTE FALLS HEALTH CENTER BCCTP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Medical benefit program through OHP for individuals needing treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer who do not have health insurance coverage. Individuals may ask their provider to refer them to the program. 722 Laurel Avenue Butte Falls, OR 97522 Eligibility: People under age 65 who live in Oregon and: -Need treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer -Do not have minimum essential coverage for treatment -Do not qualify for any other Medicaid programs -Household income at or below 250% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) -Anyone with a diagnosis of breast or cervical cancer to apply. The program does not look at gender identity or expression. Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 773-3863 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE COMMUNITY HEALTH MEDFORD HEALTH CENTER BCCTP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Medical benefit program through OHP for individuals needing treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer who do not have health insurance coverage. Individuals may ask their provider to refer them to the program. 19 Myrtle Street Medford, OR 97504 Eligibility: People under age 65 who live in Oregon and: -Need treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer -Do not have minimum essential coverage for treatment -Do not qualify for any other Medicaid programs -Household income at or below 250% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) -Anyone with a diagnosis of breast or cervical cancer to apply. The program does not look at gender identity or expression. Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 773-3863 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROGUE COMMUNITY HEALTH WHITE CITY HEALTH CENTER BCCTP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Medical benefit program through OHP for individuals needing treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer who do not have health insurance coverage. Individuals may ask their provider to refer them to the program. 8385 Division Road White City, OR 975031176 Eligibility: People under age 65 who live in Oregon and: -Need treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer -Do not have minimum essential coverage for treatment -Do not qualify for any other Medicaid programs -Household income at or below 250% Federal Poverty Level (FPL) -Anyone with a diagnosis of breast or cervical cancer to apply. The program does not look at gender identity or expression. Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 773-3863 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SILVERTON AREA COMMUNITY AID FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency rent and utility assistance (electric, water, sewer, gas, and propane). Assistance is dependent on available funds, and clients are encouraged to contribute if they can. 1030 N 1st Street Suite A Silverton, OR 97381 Eligibility: Households must meet Silverton Area Community Aid Income Guidelines Hours: Monday 11am-2pm, Tuesday 4pm-6pm, Thursday 9am-noon Email (503) 873-3446 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST LUKE CATHOLIC CHURCH FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides financial assistance with medical care, prescriptions, past due electric, natural gas, and water bills. Does not provide assistance with gasoline, rent, or propane. Assistance is dependent upon available funding and is not guaranteed. This resource may only be accessed once in a 12 month period. 417 Harrison Street Woodburn, OR 97071 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: By appointment only (971) 338-1074 Get Directions Visit Website More Details SWEET RELIEF SWEET RELIEF MUSICAN FUND Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides emergency financial assistance. 2650 E Imperial Highway Suite 208 Brea, CA 92821 Eligibility: Individuals who are professional musicians or music industry workers who have recently had a serious medical or mental condition that affects applicant's ability to work and can show that 50% of their income comes from said industry Hours: No standard hours Email (714) 626-0447 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VIRGINIA GARCIA MEMORIAL HEALTH CENTER 7TH AVENUE ACUTE CARE CLINIC BCCTP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Medical benefit program through OHP for individuals needing treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer who do not have health insurance coverage. Individuals may ask their provider to refer them to the program. 232 SE 7th Avenue Hillsboro, OR 97123 Eligibility: People under age 65 who live in Oregon and: -Need treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer, -Do not have minimum essential coverage for treatment, -Do not qualify for any other Medicaid programs, and -Household income at or below 250% Federal Poverty Level (FPL). -Anyone with a diagnosis of breast or cervical cancer to apply. The program does not look at gender identity or expression. Hours: No standard hours (503) 359-5564 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VIRGINIA GARCIA MEMORIAL HEALTH CENTER BEAVERTON WELLNESS CENTER BCCTP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Medical benefit program through OHP for individuals needing treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer who do not have health insurance coverage. Individuals may ask their provider to refer them to the program. 2725 SW Cedar Hills Boulevard Suite 200 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: People under age 65 who live in Oregon and: -Need treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer, -Do not have minimum essential coverage for treatment, -Do not qualify for any other Medicaid programs, and -Household income at or below 250% Federal Poverty Level (FPL). -Anyone with a diagnosis of breast or cervical cancer to apply. The program does not look at gender identity or expression. Hours: No standard hours (503) 352-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VIRGINIA GARCIA MEMORIAL HEALTH CENTER CORNELIUS WELLNESS CENTER BCCTP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Medical benefit program through OHP for individuals needing treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer who do not have health insurance coverage. Individuals may ask their provider to refer them to the program. 1151 N Adair Street Cornelius, OR 97113 Eligibility: People under age 65 who live in Oregon and: -Need treatment for breast cancer or cervical cancer, -Do not have minimum essential coverage for treatment, -Do not qualify for any other Medicaid programs, and -Household income at or below 250% Federal Poverty Level (FPL). -Anyone with a diagnosis of breast or cervical cancer to apply. The program does not look at gender identity or expression. Hours: No standard hours (503) 359-5564 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WE CARE EMERGENCY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides limited financial assistance for emergency needs such as past-due rent, rental deposits, electricity, gas, garbage, water, and small non-covered medical bills. May also assist with minor car repairs with safety issues and other miscellaneous emergencies. Prospective clients must be able to demonstrate the ability to sustain needs post-assistance. Individuals who have not previously received aid from the agency may be given application priority. 602 SW Madison Avenue Corvallis, OR 97333 Eligibility: -Unrestricted for most assistance -Individuals experiencing homelessness and living in car for vehicle expenses Hours: Phone and email: Monday-Wednesday 10am-4pm Church office: Varies Email (541) 203-0102 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV CASE MANAGEMENT CAREASSIST CAREASSIST DESCHUTES COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV/AIDS CASE MANAGEMENT HAYDEN'S HELPING HANDS HAYDEN'S HELPING HANDS HOOD RIVER COUNTY HEALTH DEPARTMENT HIV/AIDS CASE MANAGEMENT KAISER PERMANENTE HEALTH CARE MEDICAL FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM LINN COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH HIV/AIDS RAPID TESTING AND PREVENTION NORTHWEST FAMILY SERVICES WOMEN'S HEALTH PROGRAM OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON FAMILY TO FAMILY HEALTH INFORMATION CENTER OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION BREAST AND CERVICAL CANCER TREATMENT OREGON LIONS SIGHT AND HEARING FOUNDATION PATIENT CARE PROGRAM ROGUE COMMUNITY HEALTH BCCTP ROGUE COMMUNITY HEALTH BCCTP ROGUE COMMUNITY HEALTH BCCTP ROGUE COMMUNITY HEALTH BCCTP SILVERTON AREA COMMUNITY AID FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE ST LUKE CATHOLIC CHURCH FAMILY SERVICES VIRGINIA GARCIA MEMORIAL HEALTH CENTER BCCTP VIRGINIA GARCIA MEMORIAL HEALTH CENTER BCCTP VIRGINIA GARCIA MEMORIAL HEALTH CENTER BCCTP WE CARE EMERGENCY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE
